---
title: "Build a design matrix"
subtitle: "Model event timing with a typed spec and inspect the regressors."
jupyter: python3
---

## Create Events

```{python}
import pandas as pd
import fmrimod as fm
from fmrimod.spec import hrf, compile as compile_spec
from fmrimod.design_colmap import design_colmap

events = pd.DataFrame({
    "onset": [0, 8, 16, 24, 32, 40],
    "condition": ["face", "scene", "face", "scene", "face", "scene"],
    "response_time": [0.8, 1.1, 0.7, 0.9, 1.0, 1.2],
    "duration": 2.0,
})
events
```

## Define the Acquisition Grid

```{python}
sf = fm.SamplingFrame(blocklens=60, TR=1.0)
sf.samples[:8]
```

## Build the Design from a Typed Spec

`hrf(...)` is a typed value. `compile()` lowers a spec against the events
and sampling frame into an `EventModel` (and its baseline) — no fitting,
no string formula. The design matrix and column metadata are inspectable
immediately.

```{python}
spec = hrf("condition", basis="spmg1")
event_model, baseline_model = compile_spec(spec, data=events, sampling_frame=sf)

event_model.design_matrix.shape
```

```{python}
event_model.column_names
```

## Inspect Column Metadata

`design_colmap` reports the provenance of every column — which condition,
which basis, what role — for the compiled event model.

```{python}
design_colmap(event_model)[["col", "name", "condition", "role", "basis_name"]]
```

## Add a Parametric Modulator

To model a condition modulated by a trial-by-trial continuous variable
(reaction time, accuracy, confidence, ...), use the typed-spec
`modulators=` keyword. This expands the term into an unmodulated
boxcar plus one regressor per modulator. By default each modulator
is mean-centered at the raw-value level (pre-convolution), giving
the unmodulated boxcar and modulated columns the near-orthogonal
structure that makes per-modulator betas interpretable.

```{python}
modulated_spec = hrf("condition", basis="spmg1", modulators=("response_time",))
mod_model = fm.event_model(modulated_spec, data=events, sampling_frame=sf)

design_colmap(mod_model)[["col", "name", "condition", "role", "basis_name"]]
```

The added `response_time` regressor appears as its own column, tagged
with where it came from. Because the modulator is part of the typed
term metadata rather than a formula-string suffix, you can read off
exactly which column belongs to which term before fitting anything.

To see a design like this carried through `fmri_lm`, a contrast, and
into a group analysis, follow the **[golden path](golden-path.qmd)**.

See the [parametric modulators](parametric-modulators.qmd) tutorial
for the full story on centering semantics, the `center_modulators=`
opt-out, and why fmrimod does not orthogonalize modulators
automatically.

## Next

- [The golden path](golden-path.qmd) — the whole workflow into group, with a parity check
- [Linear model](linear-model.qmd)
- [Contrasts](contrasts.qmd)
- [Overview and workflow](overview-workflow.qmd)
